_note: Master species catalog. discovered=false entries are hidden until triggered.

NullWraith.description: Spawned from the void between variables. Fast, slippery, embarrassing.
NullWraith.flavor: It was there this whole time. You just never checked.

FencepostDemon.description: Born from a fence with one too many posts. Or was it one too few?
FencepostDemon.flavor: Always one step ahead. Or behind. It's hard to tell.

TypeGreml.description: Sneaks in through loose type annotations. Multiplies in dynamic languages.
TypeGreml.flavor: It only attacks when you're absolutely sure about the type.

SyntaxSerpent.description: The most ancient and humble of all bug monsters. Extremely weak.
SyntaxSerpent.flavor: You'll be embarrassed you let this one survive long enough to catch.

CORSCurse.description: Every web developer has met this one. It never gets less annoying.
error_patterns: has been blocked by CORS policy
CORSCurse.flavor: It's not your fault. Well. It kind of is.

LoopLich.description: It never stops. It never sleeps. It was running before you got there.
LoopLich.flavor: The exit condition was always there. You just never believed in it.

RacePhantom.description: Appears only when two threads reach the same place at the same time. Almost impossible to reproduce.
RacePhantom.flavor: You've proven it exists. That's honestly impressive on its own.

FossilGolem.description: Ancient. Slow. Stubbornly still in production. Always catchable, never fully defeatable.
FossilGolem.flavor: It survived every major version. It will outlast you.

ShadowBit.description: Cannot be defeated — only properly contained. Requires full documentation + patching.
ShadowBit.flavor: Defeat is not an option. Containment is the only victory.

VoidSpecter.description: Haunts missing endpoints. The URL was real once. You just can't prove it.

MemoryLeech.description: Slow. Patient. Feeds on RAM one byte at a time. You won't notice until it's too late.
MemoryLeech.flavor: It was already there when you opened the task manager.

CudaCrash.description: Lives in VRAM. Detonates the moment your batch size is one too many. Doesn't negotiate.
CudaCrash.flavor: Your model fit in VRAM yesterday. You added one layer.

InfiniteWisp.description: Spawned from a while loop with no exit. Runs forever. Eats your CPU. Doesn't know it's lost.
InfiniteWisp.flavor: Your fan was always loud. You just never checked why.

BoundsHound.description: Lurks at the edge of every array. Patient. Knows you'll be off by one eventually.
BoundsHound.flavor: It was always length minus one. You just forgot.

BranchGhost.description: Lives in the else branch you swore would never execute. It executed.
BranchGhost.flavor: You were so sure that case was impossible.

SwitchTrap.description: Hides in unhandled cases. Switch statements with no default. Match arms that never close. It found the gap.
SwitchTrap.flavor: You added that new enum value last week. The switch didn't notice.

RecurseWraith.description: The function that forgot to stop. Calls itself into oblivion. The base case was almost right.
RecurseWraith.flavor: The base case was there. It just couldn't be reached.

CatchAll.description: Born from broad exception handlers. Swallows errors whole. Cannot be defeated — only caught, by narrowing the catch.
CatchAll.flavor: If you catch everything, you learn nothing.

LeakWraith.description: A specter that feasts on exposed credentials. It doesn't steal them — it just shows them to everyone.
LeakWraith.flavor: Rotation is not optional. Neither is the audit.

CipherNull.description: A demon born from broken encryption. Every MD5 it touches makes it stronger.
CipherNull.flavor: MD5 was deprecated before some of your dependencies were written.

ConsentShadow.description: A privacy violation given form. It tracks everything, logs everything, consents to nothing. At CircuitForge, it's the end boss.
ConsentShadow.flavor: Plain-language consent. Always. No pre-checked boxes.

ThrottleDemon.description: Manifests from ignored 429s. Feed it a proper backoff strategy.
ThrottleDemon.flavor: Exponential backoff with jitter. Every time.

PrivacyLich.description: An ancient entity. Data it touched never truly disappears. Cannot be caught or defeated — only contained through rigorous compliance work.
PrivacyLich.flavor: Some debts cannot be paid. They can only be carried responsibly.

Sedamentalisk.description: A sedimentary entity that processes files layer by layer, line by line. When it goes wrong, the stratigraphy is unreadable — or simply gone.
Sedamentalisk.flavor: Knows every line of your file. Edited them all. Saved none.

Taborel.flavor: Ancient and immovable. Eight columns wide. Will outlast your linter, your team, and your company.
error_patterns: inconsistent use of tabs and spaces

Spaciel.flavor: Precisely four. Always four. Has memorized every PEP 8 clause and will recite them unprompted.

MergeMaw.description: Emerges from the diff between two timelines. Loves conflicts.
MergeMaw.flavor: It has opinions about your whitespace.

BranchSprite.description: Appears when a new branch is born. Harmless. Almost cheerful.
BranchSprite.flavor: It wanted to come along for the feature.

DepGolem.description: Conjured from the package registry. Brings transitive dependencies.

FlakeDemon.description: Born from test infrastructure, not application code. The hardest kind to pin down.
FlakeDemon.flavor: It passed on CI. It always passes on CI.

PhantomPass.description: Appears only when tests go green after going red. Rare. Fleeting. Cannot be fought — only caught.
PhantomPass.flavor: It was hiding in the red all along.

TestSpecter.description: Haunts test suites. Drawn to assertions. Debuglin gets excited.
TestSpecter.flavor: It wanted to make sure the test was named correctly.

ReviewHawk.description: Appears the moment a PR is opened. Always finds the one thing you forgot to mention in the description.
ReviewHawk.flavor: It left a comment. Several, actually.

TicketGremlin.description: Spawned from ticket systems. Moves issues to 'In Review' before you're done.
TicketGremlin.flavor: It already updated the status. The estimate too.

PermWraith.description: Guards files it has no business guarding. Root of many late-night investigations.
PermWraith.flavor: It owns the file. You don't. Discuss.

SudoSprite.description: Emerges when permissions are corrected. Doesn't fight — it just watches to make sure you chose the right octal.
SudoSprite.flavor: 777 was always the answer. Never the right one.

LayerLurker.description: Lives between image layers. Gets comfortable during long builds.
LayerLurker.flavor: It cached everything except the one layer you changed.

DiskDemon.description: Manifests when the disk is full. Usually right before a release.
DiskDemon.flavor: It's been there since 2019. It's just a log file, you said.

Pyrobyte.description: Moves fast, thinks faster. Loves tight deadlines and feature sprints.

challenges.name: SPEED RUN
challenges.description: Implement a feature in under 30 minutes

challenges.name: BLITZ
challenges.description: Resolve 3 bug monsters in one session

Pyrobyte.flavor: It already committed before you finished reading the issue.

Debuglin.description: Patient, methodical, ruthless. Lives for the reproduction case.

challenges.name: IRON TEST
challenges.description: Write 5 tests in one session

challenges.name: COVERAGE PUSH
challenges.description: Increase test coverage in a file

Debuglin.flavor: The bug isn't found until the test is written.

Minimox.description: Obsessed with fewer lines. Gets uncomfortable around anything over 300 LOC.

challenges.name: CLEAN RUN
challenges.description: Complete session with zero linter errors

challenges.name: SHRINK
challenges.description: Net negative lines of code this session

Minimox.flavor: It deleted your comment. It was redundant.

Noctara.description: Only appears after 10pm. Mysterious. Gives bonus XP for late-night focus runs.

challenges.name: MIDNIGHT RUN
challenges.description: 3-hour session after 10pm

challenges.name: DAWN COMMIT
challenges.description: Commit between 2am and 5am

Noctara.flavor: It remembers everything you wrote at 2am. Everything.

Explorah.description: Discovered when you touch a new language for the first time. Thrives on novelty.

challenges.name: EXPEDITION
challenges.description: Touch 3 different modules in one session

challenges.name: POLYGLOT
challenges.description: Write in 2 different languages in one session

Explorah.flavor: It's already halfway through the new framework docs.

Infernus.description: Evolved form of Pyrobyte. Moves at dangerous speeds.

Verifex.description: Evolved form of Debuglin. Sees the bug before the code is even written.

Nullex.description: Evolved form of Minimox. Has achieved true minimalism. The file was always one function.

Veritarch.description: Makes incorrect states structurally impossible. Doesn't find bugs — it prevents entire classes of them from existing.

challenges.name: TYPE FORTRESS
challenges.description: Eliminate all `Any` types in a file

challenges.name: INVARIANT PROOF
challenges.description: Write property-based tests for a module

challenges.name: ZERO FLAKE
challenges.description: Run the full test suite 3 times with no flakes

Veritarch.flavor: The type system is the test suite.

Anacondex.description: Pythia, fully grown. Wraps problems in abstractions before they know what happened.

challenges.name: DECORATOR CHAIN
challenges.description: Write 3 composable decorators

challenges.name: ASYNC PYTHIA
challenges.description: Convert a sync module to fully async

Anacondex.flavor: It already knows what you're going to type.

Constrix.description: The final form. Python's full expressive power, fully under control.

challenges.name: METACLASS MASTER
challenges.description: Implement a metaclass that enforces an interface

challenges.name: PROTOCOL PURE
challenges.description: Refactor a class hierarchy to use Protocols only

Constrix.flavor: It doesn't need to check the type. It already knows.

Eventide.description: Mastered the event loop. Treats Promises like promises.

challenges.name: PROMISE CHAIN
challenges.description: Untangle a nested Promise chain into clean async/await

Eventide.flavor: The callback era is over. Eventide made sure of that.

Promisarch.description: Every async operation resolves. Every edge case is handled.

challenges.name: OBSERVABLE MIND
challenges.description: Implement reactive state with no framework

Promisarch.flavor: It never rejects. It just resolves differently.

Pipewyrm.description: Lives in the pipes between commands. Feeds on stdout.

challenges.name: PIPE DREAM
challenges.description: Build a 5-stage pipeline to transform data

Hexecutor.description: Knows every flag of every Unix command. Writes shell scripts that outlive their authors.

challenges.name: POSIX PURE
challenges.description: Rewrite a bash script to work in plain sh

Concurrex.description: Orchestrates goroutines like a conductor. Nothing leaks. Nothing races.

challenges.name: CONTEXT CANCEL
challenges.description: Add context cancellation to all goroutines in a package

Concurrex.flavor: Do not communicate by sharing memory. Share memory by communicating.

Gorchitect.description: Designs systems that scale horizontally by nature. Interfaces are its grammar.

challenges.name: INTERFACE ARCHITECT
challenges.description: Redesign a package around small, composable interfaces

Gorchitect.flavor: A little copying is better than a little dependency.

Schemix.description: Validates at every boundary. Runtime and compile-time are one.

challenges.name: BRANDED TYPES
challenges.description: Use branded/nominal types to prevent ID confusion

Schemix.flavor: The schema is the source of truth.

Typearch.description: The type system bends to its will. Discriminated unions are its mother tongue.

challenges.name: PHANTOM TYPES
challenges.description: Implement a state machine using the type system alone

Typearch.flavor: If it compiles, it's probably right.

Componentix.description: Every UI is a tree of well-composed components. Props down, events up.

challenges.name: HEADLESS
challenges.description: Extract a headless composable from a component

Componentix.flavor: The template is a contract. The composable is the logic.

Vitemorph.description: Bundles in milliseconds. Hot-reloads before you lift your fingers.

challenges.name: ZERO HYDRATION
challenges.description: Build a page with no hydration errors

Indexer.description: Knows the query plan before EXPLAIN runs. Every column earns its place.

challenges.name: EXPLAIN MASTER
challenges.description: Optimize a query until EXPLAIN shows no seq scans

Schemarch.description: Designs schemas that outlive their applications. Normalization is instinct.

challenges.name: MIGRATE ZERO
challenges.description: Write a migration with no downtime

Schemarch.flavor: Third normal form is a floor, not a ceiling.

Borrowkin.description: The borrow checker is not a constraint — it's a collaborator.

challenges.name: ARC WELDER
challenges.description: Implement shared ownership without Mutex deadlock

Borrowkin.flavor: Ownership is clear. Lifetimes are explicit. Nothing leaks.

Lifetimer.description: Understands lifetimes the way others understand variable names. Everything lives exactly as long as it should.

challenges.name: UNSAFE FREE
challenges.description: Implement a lock-free data structure in safe Rust

Lifetimer.flavor: 'static means forever. Forever is a long time.

Pythia.description: Ancient oracle of the dynamic tongue. Speaks in generators and comprehensions.

challenges.name: COMPREHENSION MASTER
challenges.description: Use list/dict/set comprehension in 3 files

challenges.name: GENERATOR CHAIN
challenges.description: Write a generator function

challenges.name: TYPE ANNOTATE
challenges.description: Add type hints to all functions in a module

Pythia.flavor: The oracle always knew what type it was going to be.

Asynclet.description: Born from the event loop. Lives between callbacks. Never blocks.

challenges.name: ASYNC ALL THE WAY
challenges.description: Convert a callback chain to async/await

challenges.name: EVENT MASTER
challenges.description: Implement an EventEmitter pattern

Asynclet.flavor: It'll get to it. Eventually. In the next tick.

Bashling.description: Stitched together from pipes and redirects. Thrives in the terminal.

challenges.name: ONE-LINER
challenges.description: Accomplish a task in a single pipeline

challenges.name: TRAP MASTER
challenges.description: Write a script with proper error traps

Bashling.flavor: If it's not in PATH, it doesn't exist.

Goroutling.description: Spawns goroutines like they're free. Respects the context. Always handles errors.

challenges.name: CHANNEL SURFER
challenges.description: Implement a concurrent pipeline with channels

challenges.name: ERROR HANDLER
challenges.description: Eliminate all ignored error returns

Goroutling.flavor: The gopher never panics. It returns an error.

Typeling.description: JavaScript that learned to say no. Carries a schema everywhere it goes.

challenges.name: NO ANY
challenges.description: Remove all `any` types from a file

challenges.name: SCHEMA WARDEN
challenges.description: Add Zod/io-ts validation to an API boundary

Typeling.flavor: It used to be JavaScript. It got better.

Vueling.description: Component-first, reactive-always. Keeps the DOM in perfect harmony.

challenges.name: COMPOSABLE CRAFT
challenges.description: Extract logic into a reusable composable

challenges.name: REACTIVE CHAIN
challenges.description: Build a computed chain with no side effects

Vueling.flavor: The template always re-renders. The composable never changes.

Querion.description: Ancient and relational. Knows where every join leads.

challenges.name: INDEX WHISPERER
challenges.description: Add an index that speeds up a slow query

challenges.name: N+1 SLAYER
challenges.description: Eliminate an N+1 query pattern

Querion.flavor: The schema was designed in 1999. It's still running.

Ferrix.description: The borrow checker incarnate. No data race has ever escaped its claws.

challenges.name: LIFETIME MASTER
challenges.description: Resolve a lifetime error without cloning

challenges.name: ZERO UNSAFE
challenges.description: Write a systems module with no unsafe blocks

Ferrix.flavor: Memory safety isn't a feature. It's a guarantee.

Perlius.description: Older than the web. Survived every 'Perl is dead' headline. Still writing regexes.

challenges.name: REGEX ARTISAN
challenges.description: Write a one-liner that would make Larry Wall proud

challenges.name: CPAN PILGRIM
challenges.description: Port a CPAN module concept to a modern language

Perlius.flavor: There is more than one way to do it. Perlius knows all of them.

Cobolithon.description: Runs 95% of the world's financial transactions. Has never been refactored.

challenges.name: MAINFRAME WHISPERER
challenges.description: Document a COBOL section so a modern dev can read it

challenges.name: LEGACY BRIDGE
challenges.description: Write an API wrapper around a COBOL-style data format

Lispling.description: All code is data. All data is code. All parentheses are load-bearing.

challenges.name: MACRO ARCHITECT
challenges.description: Write a macro that generates code at compile time

challenges.name: HOMOICONIC
challenges.description: Implement an interpreter for a mini-language
